hypothesis: An MLIP that integrates a differentiable long-range electrostatics correction, learned jointly with a local equivariant graph neural network potential and trained on a dataset enriched with charged species, will surpass charge-agnostic, short-range MLIPs in predicting energies, forces, and dipole moments for ionic and polar systems and will enable stable, long-timescale MD of ionic solutions beyond current SPICE2-based benchmarks.
